git add scripts/ci/retry.sh .github/workflows/deploy.yml && git commit -q -m "ci(deploy): retry + verify the flaky az/registry steps

3/3 Deploy runs on 2026-07-02 needed a manual frontend retry, two different
transients: az containerapp update crashing on a masked transient ARM error
(the extension's _polish_bad_errors raises KeyError: 'properties' — Deploys
#1/#2, likely env-busy 409/429 right after the backend rolls on the same ACA
env) and a Docker Hub 502 pulling node:22-alpine before the push (Deploy #3).

- scripts/ci/retry.sh — shared sourced helper: 3 attempts, 30s/60s backoff,
  ::warning:: per attempt. Everything wrapped is idempotent to re-run.
- Both image build+push steps retried (rebuild hits the layer cache).
- All four az containerapp mutations retried (api/worker/frontend update +
  migrate job update, and the job start via an inline capture loop).
- api/worker/frontend rolls now VERIFIED post-update against the live
  template image — the extension can crash while rendering a response, so
  exit code alone doesn't prove the roll took.
- Frontend keeps one extra last-resort attempt with --debug so the masked
  ARM response finally lands in the log if the flake persists.

Fixes #539

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -qu origin ci/issue-539-deploy-retries 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title "ci(deploy): retry + verify the flaky az/registry steps (Fixes #539)" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
**Fixes #539** — 3/3 Deploy runs on 2026-07-02 needed a manual frontend retry, from two different transients (az containerapp extension \`KeyError: 'properties'\` masking an ARM error ×2; Docker Hub 502 on the base pull ×1).

- **\`scripts/ci/retry.sh\`** — shared sourced helper (3 attempts, 30/60s backoff, \`::warning::\` annotations). No third-party retry action.
- **Retried:** both image build+push steps (layer cache makes re-runs cheap) and all four \`az containerapp\` mutations (api / worker / frontend update, migrate **job update** + **job start**).
- **Verified:** the three app rolls now assert the live template image equals the pushed tag post-update — the extension can crash while *rendering* a response, so exit code alone doesn't prove the roll took.
- **Debuggable:** the frontend step's last-resort attempt runs with \`--debug\` so the masked ARM response finally lands in the log if the flake persists.
- Deliberately NOT done: base-image mirror swap (once-off 502), extension pin (backend's identical command passes in the same runs → not version drift), third-party retry action (supply-chain surface).

## Test plan
- \`bash -n\` + YAML parse + a stubbed self-test of \`retry\` (transient→success, persistent→rc 1) locally.
- Real validation: a \`workflow_dispatch\` Deploy run after merge (green run proves non-breakage; the flake itself is intermittent).

content.text:
Review target: `544`

`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

+          [[ "$live" == "$IMAGE" ]] || { echo "::error::API image mismatch after update: $live"; exit 1; }
 
       - name: Deploy worker
         run: |
-          az containerapp update \
+          set -euo pipefail
+          source scripts/ci/retry.sh
+          retry az containerapp update \
             --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
             --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
             --image "$IMAGE"
+          live=$(az containerapp show \
+            --name "${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
+            --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
+            --query "properties.template.containers[0].image" -o tsv)
+          [[ "$live" == "$IMAGE" ]] || { echo "::error::worker image mismatch after update: $live"; exit 1; }
 
   frontend:
     name: Frontend — build image, deploy to Container App
@@ -173,12 +209,33 @@ jobs:
       # publish-images.yml for the BYOL/eval path.
       - name: Build + push frontend image to GHCR
         run: |
-          docker build -f frontend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE_FRONTEND" frontend
-          docker push "$IMAGE_FRONTEND"
-
+          set -euo pipefail
+          source scripts/ci/retry.sh
+          build_push() {
+            docker build -f frontend/Dockerfile -t "$IMAGE_FRONTEND" frontend
+            docker push "$IMAGE_FRONTEND"
+          }
+          retry build_push
+
+      # Retried + verified like the API/worker rolls; on the third failure one
+      # last attempt runs with --debug so the ARM response the extension masks
+      # (KeyError: 'properties', #539 — Deploys #1/#2) finally lands in the log.
       - name: Deploy frontend
         run: |
-          az containerapp update \
+          set -euo pipefail
+          source scripts/ci/retry.sh
+          update() {
+            az containerapp update \
+              --name "${{ vars.FRONTEND_APP_NAME }}" \
+              --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
+              --image "$IMAGE_FRONTEND" "$@"
+          }
+          if ! retry update; then
+            echo "::warning::final attempt with --debug to surface the masked ARM error (#539)"
+            update --debug
+          fi
+          live=$(az containerapp show \
             --name "${{ vars.FRONTEND_APP_NAME }}" \
             --resource-group "${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
-            --image "$IMAGE_FRONTEND"
+            --query "properties.template.containers[0].image" -o tsv)
+          [[ "$live" == "$IMAGE_FRONTEND" ]] || { echo "::error::frontend image mismatch after update: $live"; exit 1; }
diff --git a/scripts/ci/retry.sh b/scripts/ci/retry.sh
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..27cf66d0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/scripts/ci/retry.sh
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+# Shared CI retry helper (#539) — source this, don't execute it.
+#
+# Both Deploy-workflow transients this covers are idempotent to re-run:
+#   - `az containerapp update/job update` crashing on a transient ARM error
+#     (the containerapp extension's `_polish_bad_errors` masks 409/429-style
+#     responses as `KeyError: 'properties'` — Deploys #1/#2, 2026-07-02)
+#   - registry blips during image build/push (Docker Hub 502 on the base-image
+#     pull — Deploy #3)
+#
+# Usage:  retry <cmd> [args…]        # 3 attempts, 30s/60s backoff
+# The command's exit code is propagated from the final attempt.
+
+retry() {
+  local attempt
+  for attempt in 1 2 3; do
+    "$@" && return 0
+    if (( attempt < 3 )); then
+      echo "::warning::'$*' failed (attempt ${attempt}/3) — retrying in $(( attempt * 30 ))s (#539)"
+      sleep $(( attempt * 30 ))
+    fi
+  done
+  echo "::error::'$*' failed after 3 attempts (#539)"
+  return 1
+}

input.new_string:
steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v7

      # Fail fast on unset repo vars (#539 post-mortem): an empty --name reaches
      # ARM and the containerapp extension masks the 4xx as KeyError:
      # 'properties' — four deploys failed before --debug exposed that
      # FRONTEND_APP_NAME was simply never configured. Never let an empty var
      # masquerade as a transient again.
      - name: Preflight — required repo vars
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          fail=0
          for pair in "AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP=${{ vars.AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP }}" \
                      "API_APP_NAME=${{ vars.API_APP_NAME }}" \
                      "WORKER_APP_NAME=${{ vars.WORKER_APP_NAME }}" \
                      "MIGRATE_JOB_NAME=${{ vars.MIGRATE_JOB_NAME }}"; do
            [[ -n "${pair#*=}" ]] || { echo "::error::repo variable ${pair%%=*} is not set"; fail=1; }
          done
          exit $fail

      - name: Azure login (OIDC)
        uses: azure/login@v3
        with:
          client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
          tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
          subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      # Push to GHCR (ADR 0023). The GITHUB_TOKEN has packages:write on this repo's
      # registry; no Azure/ACR credential is involved in the image push.

Deploy #4 proved the frontend failures were never a flake: --debug showed
az containerapp update running with --name '' — vars.FRONTEND_APP_NAME was
never configured after the SWA->Container-App cutover (#509), and the
extension masks the resulting ARM 4xx as KeyError: 'properties'. The var is
now set; this guard makes any future unset var fail the job in seconds with
its NAME instead of burning a build + masked ARM errors.

Refs #539

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -qu origin ci/deploy-preflight-vars 2>&1 | tail -1 && gh pr create --title "ci(deploy): preflight-fail on unset repo vars (#539 post-mortem)" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
Deploy #4 (first run with #544's retries) settled #539's root cause: **not a flake**. The \`--debug\` last-resort attempt showed \`az containerapp update --name ''\` — **\`vars.FRONTEND_APP_NAME\` was never configured** after the SWA→Container-App cutover (#509); the containerapp extension masks the resulting ARM 4xx as \`KeyError: 'properties'\`. Deploys #1/#2/#4 all died on it; only #3's Docker Hub 502 was a real transient.

- The missing repo variable is now set (\`FRONTEND_APP_NAME=dataq-app-frontend\`).
- This PR adds a **preflight step to both jobs** that fails in seconds naming any unset required var — an empty \`--name\` can never masquerade as a transient ARM error again.
- #544's retry+verify hardening stays valuable for the true transients (registry blips, real ARM 409/429s) and just proved its diagnostic worth — the \`--debug\` tail is what exposed this.

Refs #539

## Test plan
- YAML parses; bash logic mirrors the tested #544 patterns.
- Real validation: Deploy #5 after merge — expecting the frontend job's first CI success since the cutover.

content.text:
Done — **Deploy #5 is fully green, including the frontend job's first CI success since the SWA→Container-App cutover.** All three apps (`api`, `worker`, `frontend`) verified on the new `a358ac60` image, `/healthz` ok, and no retries were needed — clean path.

The full story of what you asked for:

**#539 — fixed, and it wasn't what we thought.** The retry hardening (**#544**: shared `scripts/ci/retry.sh`, retry on all `az containerapp` mutations + both image builds, post-roll image verification, `--debug` last resort) went in first — and on its very first run, the `--debug` tail exposed the real cause: `az containerapp update --name ''`. **`vars.FRONTEND_APP_NAME` was never created** when the frontend moved off SWA (#509), so deploys #1/#2/#4 failed *deterministically* with the extension masking the ARM error as `KeyError: 'properties'`; only #3's Docker Hub 502 was a true transient. Resolution: the repo variable is now set, **#546** added a preflight step to both jobs that fails in seconds naming any unset var, the post-mortem is documented on #539, and Deploy #5 proved the whole chain. The retries stay — they cover the genuine transients and just demonstrated their diagnostic value.

**CodeQL alert — closed.** **#545** hoisted the `client.delete(...)` out of the assert in the #540 regression test (`py/side-effect-in-assert`); it was the only open code-scanning alert, and it auto-closes now that the fix is on `main`.

**Epic #176 — synced** at 36/40: the Azure-Monitor webhook group is ticked (collapsed to the one live factory, smoke 4m14s), Flows A/B/C and the deferred-smoke discharge are ticked, and the status header reflects the 2026-07-02 close-out. What's left on it: team onboarding and the optional Claude-Desktop client-side NL-selection check.

content.text: Host found. Executing the rotation in one leak-safe block — only metadata and status codes are printed:

input.command:
bash <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
HOST="https://dbc-b16e0901-d85c.cloud.databricks.com"
VAULT="dataq-app-kv-aw6laj"
OLD=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name "$VAULT" -n conn-unity-catalog-retail --query value -o tsv)
echo "old token fetched from KV: ${#OLD} chars"

echo "--- existing tokens (metadata only) ---"
curl -s "$HOST/api/2.0/token/list" -H "Authorization: Bearer $OLD" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; [print(t['token_id'][:12], '|', t.get('comment','<no comment>'), '| created', __import__('datetime').datetime.fromtimestamp(t['creation_time']/1000).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'), '| expires', ('never' if t.get('expiry_time',-1)<0 else __import__('datetime').datetime.fromtimestamp(t['expiry_time']/1000).strftime('%Y-%m-%d'))) for t in json.load(sys.stdin).get('token_infos',[])]"

NEW_JSON=$(curl -s -X POST "$HOST/api/2.0/token/create" -H "Authorization: Bearer $OLD" \
  -d '{"comment":"dataq conn-unity-catalog-retail (rotated 2026-07-02, replaces leaked pre-#538 PAT)","lifetime_seconds":7776000}')
NEW=$(printf '%s' "$NEW_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token_value',''))")
NEW_ID=$(printf '%s' "$NEW_JSON" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token_info',{}).get('token_id',''))")

content.text:
Confirmed — the email is accurate, and it's the compute that's dead, not our credentials:

```
HTTP 400: "Sorry, cannot run the resource because you've exhausted your
available credits. Please add a payment method to upgrade your account."
```

**No Databricks jobs are running, and none can start.** The jobs runs list is empty, the Serverless Starter Warehouse is STOPPED, and an explicit start request is rejected with the credit-exhaustion error. The control plane (auth, token APIs, warehouse metadata) still works — which is why the token rotation completed cleanly and all three new PATs check out at HTTP 200 — but anything needing compute is blocked.

What this takes down, concretely:

- The **`flow_a_uc_reference` DAG run I triggered** as the rotation proof is stuck "running" — its `databricks-sql-connector` call is waiting on a warehouse that will never start. It'll fail on its own timeout, and the failure callback will land in DataQ as a failed pipeline run (arguably a realistic alert-path demo, but not the green proof I wanted — the rotation itself is proven by the API auth checks instead).
- **Flow B (medallion)** and the scheduled **Flow A-UC** loads will fail every cycle the same way.
- **DataQ's two UC connections** (`retail`, `qa`): connection tests and UC suite runs will error until compute is back. Snowflake and flat-file lanes are completely unaffected.

One caution worth deciding now: the harness schedules will keep re-running the two Databricks-touching DAGs, and each failure will flow into DataQ's pipeline-runs plus fire failure alerts (email/Teams) every cycle. content.text:
Here's the estimate, grounded in the actual config rather than guesswork.

**What runs on Databricks, and when.** The warehouse is the only compute: a **serverless SQL warehouse, size Small (12 DBU/hr), auto-stop 10 min**. The daily load is deliberately clustered: `flow_a_uc_reference` at 01:45 UTC, `flow_b_medallion` at 02:00, and the DataQ suite runs (Flow A-UC, silver/gold) trigger right behind each on the still-warm warehouse. That's one consolidated window of roughly **40–45 warehouse-minutes per day** (a few minutes of actual queries, the rest auto-stop idle tails).

**The math** (AWS serverless SQL list price ≈ $0.70/DBU):

| | Per day | 2 weeks |
|---|---|---|
| Scheduled window (~0.75 h × 12 DBU/h) | ~$6.30 | **~$88** |
| + ad-hoc touches (each stray connection test / dry-run / profiler call wakes the warehouse for a ~10-min tail ≈ $1.50–2) | +$1–3 | +$15–40 |
| **Realistic total** | **$7–9** | **≈ $90–130** |

Worth knowing: the trial credits burned fast because of the smoke/setup days — repeated manual runs, E2E, profiling kept the warehouse awake far more than the steady-state schedule will. Two quiet scheduled weeks are much cheaper than the past week was.

**If you want it cheaper before adding a card:** downsize the warehouse Small → 2X-Small (4 DBU/hr — the retail demo data won't notice) and drop `auto_stop_mins` from 10 to 5. Same schedules, same demo, and the two-week figure falls to roughly **$30–45**. Both are one-line API/UI changes I can make as soon as compute is billable again.

Storage and everything else are negligible here, and this is Databricks-only — Snowflake, ACA, and the rest of the Azure stack are unaffected either way.

So: adding a payment method costs you on the order of **$100 for the fortnight at current shape, or ~$35 trimmed**. If you'd rather not pay, say the word and I'll pause `flow_a_uc_reference` + `flow_b_medallion` so the dead UC lane doesn't spam failure alerts; Snowflake and flat-file lanes keep the demo alive.
stop the jobs. [can we do one time payment instead of adding credit card?]

bash <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
AF="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
PW=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name dataq-app-kv-aw6laj -n conn-airflow --query value -o tsv)
for dag in flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion; do
  curl -s -u "admin:$PW" -X PATCH "$AF/api/v1/dags/$dag" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"is_paused": true}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('dag_id'), '-> paused:', d.get('is_paused'))"
done
# Kill the stuck rotation-proof run (its warehouse will never start)
RUNS=$(curl -s -u "admin:$PW" "$AF/api/v1/dags/flow_a_uc_reference/dagRuns?state=running" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(' '.join(r['dag_run_id'] for r in json.load(sys.stdin).get('dag_runs',[])))")
for r in $RUNS; do
  curl -s -u "admin:$PW" -X PATCH "$AF/api/v1/dags/flow_a_uc_reference/dagRuns/$r" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"state": "failed"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('run', d.get('dag_run_id','?')[:40], '->', d.get('state'))"
done
echo "--- final DAG states ---"
curl -s -u "admin:$PW" "$AF/api/v1/dags?dag_id_pattern=flow" | python3 -c "import sys,json; [print(d['dag_id'], '| paused:', d['is_paused']) for d in json.load(sys.stdin)['dags']]"
EOF

content.text:
Jobs are stopped and clean: both UC-touching DAGs paused, the stuck rotation-proof run is terminal (`failed`), and the last scheduled run before the credits died was green. No more failure-alert noise, and Snowflake/flat-file/event lanes keep running untouched.

On the payment question — short answer: **there's no self-serve one-time top-up for a trial workspace**, but you have three real alternatives:

1. **AWS Marketplace billing (closest to what you want).** This workspace is AWS-hosted, and you can subscribe to Databricks pay-as-you-go *through AWS Marketplace* — no card goes to Databricks; usage lands on your existing AWS bill, and any AWS credits you hold effectively prepay it. Functionally it's still PAYG, but there's no new payment relationship and you can cancel the subscription the moment the demo ends.
2. **Card PAYG, treated as one-time.** Usage-based with no minimum — at the paused-lane-resumed rate we estimated (~$35 trimmed / ~$100 untrimmed for the fortnight), you add the card, run two weeks, then downgrade/cancel. The "commitment" is only what you consume.
3. **Databricks Free Edition ($0, some rework).** A genuinely free serverless workspace with quota limits that would fit this demo. The cost is migration effort, not money: new workspace, recreate the UC catalog + retail data, new PATs, re-point the harness env + DataQ's two UC connections. Since `dataq_setup` and the harness scripts already exist, I could script most of that — realistically a couple of hours of work.

Two caveats: prepaid "commit" deals exist but only via Databricks sales (not worth it here), and billing options shift — worth a glance at the account console's upgrade page to confirm the Marketplace path is offered for your trial account (my knowledge has a cutoff).

My recommendation: AWS Marketplace if the no-card constraint is firm, otherwise the card + the two trim levers (warehouse → 2X-Small, auto-stop → 5 min — I'll apply both the moment compute is billable). If you pick Free Edition instead, I'll plan the migration. The paused DAGs resume with one API call either way.
Can you check if all the jobs we have setup can be managed with the databricks free edition?

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Databricks Free Edition limitations related to personal access tokens, API, and SQL warehouses:

## Databricks Free Edition Limitations

Databricks Free Edition is serverless-only with per-account quotas on compute, jobs, pipelines, model serving, Lakebase projects, and apps.

### Enterprise Admin Limitations

Free Edition accounts have several restrictions including:
- One workspace and one metastore per account.
- No access to the account console or account-level APIs.
- Authentication is limited to email OTP, Sign in with Google, and Sign in with Microsoft.

### Personal Access Tokens

While the search results provide information about how personal access tokens work with SQL warehouses and APIs, they don't contain specific limitations on PAT usage in Free Edition. The results show that BI Tools can connect to Databricks SQL warehouses such as Tableau or Power BI using scoped tokens, and tokens can include "sql" scopes for SQL warehouse access.

### Fair Usage Policy

If you exceed your quota, your workspace's compute resources will be shut down and unavailable for the rest of the day (and in extreme cases, the rest of the month).

For more detailed information about specific PAT limitations in Free Edition, you may want to consult the official Databricks documentation directly.

Azure Databricks | Microsoft Learn Note Databricks Free Edition is a no-cost offering and does not include guaranteed reliability, support, or service-level agreements. Free Edition accounts include many of the latest Databricks releases, but some features may be limited or unavailable. If you would like access to the full Databricks platform, you must create a new Databricks account by starting a [free trial](free-trial). Databricks Free Edition is available at no cost to all qualified users. To keep it accessible to all, each account is subject to the Databricks fair usage policy. If you exceed your quota, your workspace's compute resources will be shut down and unavailable for the rest of the day (and in extreme cases, the rest of the month). While your compute will be unavailable, your data and settings will not be deleted. You’ll be able to resume your work when the limit resets. ## Compute limitations Free Edition users only have access to [serverless compute resources](../compute/serverless/). Custom compute configurations are not supported. Additionally, outbound internet access is restricted to a limited set of trusted domains. Free Edition includes limited [serverless GPU compute](../machine-learning/ai-runtime/), available from the notebook compute selector and subject to available capacity. | Resource | Limits | | --- | --- | | [Serverless compute for notebooks](../compute/serverless/notebooks) | Limited compute size and usage. | | [SQL warehouses](../compute/sql-warehouse/) | One SQL warehouse, limited to a `2X-Small` cluster size. | | [Jobs](../jobs/) | Max of 5 concurrent job tasks per account. | | [Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines](../ldp/configure-pipeline) | One active pipeline per pipeline type. | | [Model serving endpoints](../machine-learning/model-serving/) | - Limits on the number of active endpoints

Apps run for up to 24 hours after being started, updated, or redeployed. After that, the app is automatically stopped to help manage resource usage. You can restart the app at any time. | | [Lakebase](../oltp/projects/) | One Lakebase project per account, with scale-to-zero compute. | For a full list of serverless compute limitations, see [Serverless compute limitations](../compute/serverless/limitations). ## Unsupported features The following features are not supported in Databricks Free Edition: - R and Scala - Custom workspace storage locations - Online tables - Clean rooms - All legacy Databricks features - [Knowledge Assistant](../agents/agent-bricks/knowledge-assistant) ## Administrative limitations Free Edition accounts are meant for non-commercial use. Because of this, they have the following enterprise admin-related limits: - One workspace and one metastore per account. - No access to the account console or account-level APIs. - No compliance enforcement, security customization, or private networking configurations. - Authentication is limited to email OTP, Sign in with Google, and Sign in with Microsoft. No SSO or SCIM support. ## Additional limitations - Databricks may delete Free Edition accounts that are inactive for a prolonged period. - Free Edition accounts are not covered by the Databricks support policy or Service Level Agreement (SLA). - Free Edition accounts may not be used for commercial purposes. - Free Edition accounts cannot become Databricks Marketplace providers.

content.text:
Yes — checked against the current Free Edition limitations doc (updated 2026-06-30), and **everything our UC lane does fits**, with one thing to verify at signup and one policy caveat. Here's the workload-by-workload mapping:

| Our workload | What it needs | Free Edition says | Fits? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `flow_a_uc_reference` + `flow_b_medallion` DAGs | Airflow worker connects **in** via `databricks-sql-connector` to one SQL warehouse | One serverless SQL warehouse, **2X-Small** | ✅ — one warehouse is all we use, and 2X-Small is the downsize I recommended anyway |
| Flow B notebook step (RAW→Silver→Gold) | One job run at a time on serverless | Jobs capped at **5 concurrent tasks** | ✅ — we'd use 1 |
| DataQ UC connections (retail + qa): suite runs, dry-run, profiler | JDBC-style SQL over the same warehouse | Warehouse + workspace APIs available; only **account-level** APIs are off | ✅ — config-only change on DataQ's side (host/http_path/warehouse_id), no code |
| UC catalog/schemas/tables (retail model) | Create catalog + managed tables | One workspace + one metastore; managed storage only | ✅ — we don't use external storage; data arrives via INSERTs from the Airflow worker, so the restricted *outbound* internet doesn't touch us |
| Nightly usage volume | ~15–30 min of 2X-Small per day | Fair-use quota; compute pauses for the day if exceeded | ✅ — our footprint is tiny; heavy smoke days might hit the cap, scheduled load won't |
| R/Scala, custom compute, private networking | — | Unsupported | ✅ not used |

**The one thing to verify at signup:** the docs don't explicitly confirm **PAT support** — they restrict only account-level APIs and console auth methods (email OTP / Google / Microsoft), and workspace-level tooling is a headline use case, so PATs should work — but since every integration we have (Airflow env, DataQ secrets, my rotation recipe) authenticates with PATs, I'd create the account and mint a token as the very first step before committing to the migration. Two minutes to confirm.

**The policy caveat:** Free Edition is **non-commercial use only**, with no SLA and possible deletion after prolonged inactivity. For a portfolio/demo project that's fine; if DataQ's demo ever fronts anything commercial, it isn't.

**Migration effort if you go ahead** (~2–3 hours, mostly scripted already): new workspace → mint PATs → recreate the retail catalog/schemas + reseed (mockdata + the untracked notebook) → update `DATABRICKS_HOST`/`HTTP_PATH`/warehouse_id in harness tfvars + `secrets.sh` + the two Airflow ACA env entries → update the 3 KV secrets + the two DataQ connection configs → unpause the DAGs and re-run the Flow B verification.

Sources: [Databricks Free Edition limitations (Azure)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/getting-started/free-edition-limitations), [Databricks Free Edition limitations (AWS)](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/getting-started/free-edition-limitations)

input.content:
# Databricks migration — exhausted trial → Free Edition (2026-07-02)

> Working tracker, same convention as HARNESS_TODO.md (not git-tracked).
> Context: trial credits exhausted (compute dead, control plane alive); decision = migrate to
> **Free Edition** ($0, serverless-only, non-commercial). Old workspace:
> `dbc-b16e0901-d85c.cloud.databricks.com` (AWS trial). UC-touching DAGs are PAUSED.
> Legend: `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · 🧑 = user-only step (browser/account)

## Phase 0 — Backup (DONE — nothing else worth saving)

- [x] Inventory: **no workspace notebooks, no Databricks jobs** (Flow B transforms are pure
      SQL from the Airflow DAG via `_harness.py`; catalog/schemas are terraform-managed)
- [x] UC metadata dump → `databricks/backup-2026-07-02/` — 13 tables w/ full column schemas
      (raw: feedback, sales_events · reference: 8 dims · silver: feedback, sales · gold:
      daily_revenue), catalogs.json, warehouse.json
- [x] Table DATA deliberately not exported: regenerable (mockdata Jobs → landing zone; DAG
      re-runs rebuild raw/silver/gold; reference dims reload from `flow_a_uc_reference`) and
      compute is dead anyway (no warehouse = no SELECT). DataQ's run history lives in DataQ's
      Postgres — unaffected.
- [x] Credentials: 2026-07-02 rotation already gave each consumer its own PAT and the leaked
      one is revoked — nothing credential-shaped survives the account.

## Phase 1 — Subscription + teardown (old account)

- [x] "Cancel subscription" — **no-op**: trial has no payment method attached; nothing billable
      exists. Do NOT add a card now.
- [ ] Terraform state cleanup (run from `terraform/` with harness env): remove the dead
      workspace's resources from state so future applies don't try to reach it —
      `terraform state rm databricks_catalog.dataq databricks_schema.schemas databricks_grants.catalog`
      (destroy is pointless + impossible-ish: account deletion nukes them; state rm avoids
      provider auth against a dead host). Also `state rm` any other `databricks_*` entries
      (`terraform state list | grep databricks`).
- [ ] 🧑 Delete the old Databricks account: workspace UI → top-right profile → *Manage account*
      (or accounts.cloud.databricks.com) → Settings → **Delete account**. If the console blocks
      it, the documented fallback is a support/deletion request from the account email
      (royarijit04@outlook.com). Trial accounts with $0 payment can simply be abandoned if
      deletion is gated — nothing bills.

## Phase 2 — Free Edition account (user) + PAT gate

- [ ] 🧑 Sign up at databricks.com/learn/free-edition (email OTP / Google / Microsoft — use
      royarijit04@outlook.com for continuity). One workspace + one metastore auto-provisioned.
- [ ] 🧑→me: paste the new **workspace URL** (`https://dbc-….cloud.databricks.com`)
- [ ] **PAT GATE (decisive)**: mint a PAT — UI: Settings → Developer → Access tokens, or I run
      `POST /api/2.0/token/create` after a first OAuth login. If PATs are unavailable on Free
      Edition, STOP and re-plan (OAuth M2M would need a DataQ adapter change — post-v1).
      Store nothing in files; token goes straight to KV.

## Phase 3 — Replicate (me, scripted)

- [ ] New workspace recon: `GET /api/2.0/sql/warehouses` → the one 2X-Small serverless
      warehouse → capture `warehouse_id` + `odbc_params.path` (http_path)
- [ ] Mint 3 dedicated PATs (comments: `dataq conn-unity-catalog-retail`, `… -qa`,
      `dataq-harness`; 90d) → update KV `conn-unity-catalog-retail`, `conn-unity-catalog-qa`,
      `databricks-token-harness` (vault `dataq-app-kv-aw6laj`)
- [ ] Recreate UC: update `terraform/variables.tf` defaults (`databricks_host` L121,
      `databricks_http_path` L130) + `secrets.sh` `DATABRICKS_HOST`/`DATABRICKS_TOKEN` →
      `terraform apply` (targets: `databricks_catalog.dataq`, `databricks_schema.schemas`,
      `databricks_grants.catalog` + the Airflow ACA env rolls automatically via
      `DATABRICKS_HOST`/`HTTP_PATH`/`databricks-token` secret)
      — Free Edition nuance: grants may differ (single-user account); drop
      `databricks_grants.catalog` if it errors.
- [ ] Table DDL: raw/silver/gold tables are created by the DAG loads (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT
      EXISTS` in `_harness.py` — verify) or recreate from `backup-2026-07-02/tables_*.json`
      column schemas if any load assumes pre-existing DDL
- [ ] Restart both Airflow ACAs after the apply (env is a create-time snapshot)
- [ ] Update DataQ connections (retail + qa): PATCH config `host`/`http_path`/`warehouse_id`
      via API as dataq-admin (or Olivia for retail), then `POST /connections/{id}/test` → green
- [ ] Seed reference + first loads: unpause `flow_a_uc_reference` → trigger once → 8 dims
      land; unpause `flow_b_medallion` → trigger once → raw→silver→gold rebuild from the
      landing zone (mockdata Jobs kept running throughout — landing zone is current)

## Phase 4 — Resume + verify DataQ jobs

- [ ] DataQ UC suite runs green: `flow_a_uc_reference` suite (trigger-bound, fires with the
      DAG) + `flow_b_silver`/`flow_b_gold` + a manual run on the qa connection
- [ ] Warehouse trim while at it: Free warehouse is already 2X-Small; set `auto_stop_mins=5`
- [ ] Update memory (`project_live_connections_and_demo.md`): new host, PAT expiries,
      Free Edition constraints (fair-use quota, non-commercial, 2X-Small)
- [ ] Update `HARNESS_TODO.md` + close this tracker

## Constraints to remember (Free Edition)
- One 2X-Small serverless warehouse · 5 concurrent job tasks · fair-use quota (compute pauses
  for the DAY if exceeded — keep smoke sessions short) · non-commercial use only · no SLA ·
  account console/account-level APIs unavailable · outbound egress restricted (doesn't matter:
  data flows INTO the warehouse from the Airflow worker).

Based on the search results, here's how to delete your Databricks account trial:

## Steps to Delete Your Databricks Account

**For Paid Accounts (AWS, GCP, Azure):**

In the account console sidebar, click Settings. Click the Manage your plan dropdown, then select Cancel plan. In the confirmation dialog, click Cancel plan.

**Before cancelling, Databricks recommends you:**
Terminate all compute resources in your Databricks workspaces. Delete all Unity Catalog metastores in the account. Go to each workspace and export any data or notebooks you want to import into a different account. Delete all Databricks workspaces in your account.

**Important:** Cancelling your Databricks plan is permanent and terminates your relationship with Databricks. You cannot reactivate a cancelled Databricks plan.

## After Cancelling the Plan

If you would like to fully delete your account, reach out to your account team for assistance or file a ticket at help.databricks.com. If you need to delete your Databricks account, reach out to your account team for assistance or file a ticket at help.databricks.com.

## For Free Edition Accounts

Currently, no self-serve option exists to delete a free edition account. You may try contacting help@databricks.com for assistance with removing the account.

## Important Note

In accordance with the Databricks terms of service, any customer content contained within workspaces tied to your subscription will be deleted within 30 days of cancellation.

content.text:
Here's the current process — full deletion isn't self-serve, but you only need the self-serve half:

**Step 1 — Cancel the plan (self-serve, do this now):** go to the **account console** ([accounts.cloud.databricks.com](https://accounts.cloud.databricks.com)) → **Settings** → **Manage your plan** dropdown → **Cancel plan** → confirm. Prerequisites are already met on your side (compute gone, workspace deleted) — the one thing that can still block it is the **Unity Catalog metastore**: if cancel complains, delete the metastore first (account console → Catalog → delete metastore). Note the dialog's warning is accurate: cancellation is permanent, no reactivation.

**Step 2 — Actual account deletion (not self-serve):** Databricks only deletes the account record itself via a support ticket — file one at [help.databricks.com](https://help.databricks.com) (or email help@databricks.com) asking for account deletion, referencing the account email. Per their terms, all customer content tied to the subscription is auto-deleted **within 30 days of cancellation** regardless, so the ticket only matters if you want the login/registration itself erased.

**Practical take for our migration:** Step 1 is all that materially matters — $0 trial, no payment method, workspace gone, content auto-purges in 30 days. I wouldn't block the migration on the deletion ticket. And you can reuse **royarijit04@outlook.com** for the Free Edition signup even while the old account record lingers — Databricks shows an account picker at login when one email has multiple accounts.

So: hit **Cancel plan**, optionally file the deletion ticket, then sign up for [Free Edition](https://www.databricks.com/learn/free-edition) and send me the new workspace URL — I'll take it from the PAT gate onward.

Sources: [Manage your subscription and billing (AWS docs)](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/admin/account-settings/account), [Delete Databricks account (community)](https://community.databricks.com/t5/administration-architecture/delete-databricks-account/td-p/87187)

Creating a new one alongside them is fine; they coexist on the same email and you'll just see this picker at future logins.

After you click through, it'll provision the Free Edition workspace (takes a minute or two) and land you in it — then grab the workspace URL from the browser address bar (`https://dbc-xxxxxxxx.cloud.databricks.com/...`) and send it to me.   [[ -n "$TOK" ]] || { echo "$name mint FAILED"; exit 1; }
  az keyvault secret set --vault-name "$VAULT" -n "$name" --value "$TOK" --query "attributes.updated" -o tsv | sed "s/^/$name -> KV updated: /"
done

EOF

input.command:
bash <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
AF="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
PW=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name dataq-app-kv-aw6laj -n conn-airflow --query value -o tsv)
for dag in flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion; do
  curl -s -u "admin:$PW" -X PATCH "$AF/api/v1/dags/$dag" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"is_paused": false}' \
    | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('dag_id'), 'paused:', d.get('is_paused'))"
  curl -s -u "admin:$PW" -X POST "$AF/api/v1/dags/$dag/dagRuns" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"conf":{}}' \
    | python3 -c "import sys,json; print('  triggered:', json.load(sys.stdin).get('dag_run_id',''))"
done
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  sleep 20
  states=$(for dag in flow_a_uc_reference flow_b_medallion; do
    curl -s -u "admin:$PW" "$AF/api/v1/dags/$dag/dagRuns?limit=1&order_by=-execution_date" \
      | python3 -c "import sys,json; r=json.load(sys.stdin)['dag_runs'][0]; print('$dag='+r['state'])"
  done)
  echo "poll $i: $(echo $states | tr '\n' ' ')"
  echo "$states" | grep -q "running\|queued" || break
done
EOF

input.command:
S=frontend/e2e-live/.auth/session.json
RT=$(python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('$S'))
u=[v for k,v in d.items() if k.startswith('oidc.user:')][0]
print(json.loads(u).get('refresh_token',''))")
until false; do
  AT=$(curl -s -X POST "https://login.microsoftonline.com/07b592a3-12ee-4631-abb1-8a7b73d91241/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
    -H "Origin: https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io" \
    -d "client_id=09214fa7-5978-4739-a46b-cfb669464a22&grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=$RT" 2>/dev/null \
    | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access_token',''))" 2>/dev/null)
  ROWS=$(curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $AT" "https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1/pipeline_runs?provider=airflow&limit=6" 2>/dev/null \
    | python3 -c "
import sys,json
try:
    rs=json.load(sys.stdin)
    hits=[r for r in rs if r.get('status')=='succeeded' and r.get('pipeline_or_dag_id') in ('flow_a_uc_reference','flow_b_medallion')]
    for r in hits: print('INGESTED', r['pipeline_or_dag_id'], r.get('finished_at','')[:19])
except Exception as e: print('ERR', e)
" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$ROWS" ]; then echo "$ROWS"; break; fi
  sleep 45
done

input.command:
bash <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
HOST="https://dbc-4492dde4-090c.cloud.databricks.com"
OT=$(databricks auth token --profile dataq-free | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['access_token'])")
curl -s "$HOST/api/2.0/sql/history/queries?max_results=40" -H "Authorization: Bearer $OT" | python3 -c "
import sys, json, datetime
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
seen = []
for q in d.get('res', []):
    t = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(q.get('query_start_time_ms', 0)/1000).strftime('%H:%M:%S')
    txt = ' '.join((q.get('query_text') or '').split())[:90]
    seen.append((t, q.get('status'), txt))
for t, st, txt in sorted(seen)[-18:]:
    print(t, '|', st, '|', txt)"
EOF

content.text:
**Migration complete — the UC lane is fully back on Databricks Free Edition at $0, end-to-end verified.** The final proof came straight from the prod DB: at **22:47:11Z** both trigger-bound DataQ suite runs (`airflow:flow_a_uc_reference:…` and `airflow:flow_b_medallion:…`) **succeeded** against the new warehouse — DAG load → success callback → DataQ ingest → auto-triggered GX suite run, the whole chain on the new workspace.

The full journey, as you sequenced it:

1. **Backup** — turned out minimal by design: no notebooks or Databricks jobs existed (Flow B is pure SQL from Airflow), so I dumped the 13 tables' column schemas + configs to `databricks/backup-2026-07-02/`; data was regenerable, DataQ's history lives in its own Postgres.
2. **Cancel + teardown** — nothing billable existed ($0 trial); you cancelled the plan and deleted the workspace; I cleaned the 8 dead resources out of the harness terraform state.
3. **Free Edition + PAT gate** — new workspace `dbc-4492dde4-090c`, CLI OAuth profile `dataq-free`, and the decisive check passed: **Free Edition mints PATs**.
4. **Replicate** — 3 dedicated 90-day PATs → the 3 KV secrets; one Free Edition quirk handled: Default Storage rejects catalog creation via TF/API, so `dataq_retail` + 4 schemas went in **via SQL** then `terraform import` — the harness apply is now fully clean (grants included) and rolled both Airflow apps to the new host/path/token; both DataQ connections PATCHed and testing `{ok:true}`.
5. **Resume** — DAGs unpaused, reseed runs green (dims + raw→silver→gold rebuilt), schedules live again, and the trigger-bound DataQ runs above sealed the verification.

Worth remembering: the warehouse is the fixed 2X-Small with config locked (auto-stop stays 10 min — irrelevant at $0), the fair-use quota pauses compute for the *day* if a heavy smoke session blows through it, and the account is non-commercial-only. Everything — new host, PAT expiries (~2026-09-30), the SQL-not-TF catalog quirk, the CLI profile — is recorded in memory and the closed `DATABRICKS_MIGRATION_TODO.md`.

One loose end from earlier tonight for whenever you feel like it: the optional Databricks account-record deletion ticket at help.databricks.com (the old plan is cancelled and bills nothing either way).

Finish the Databricks Free Edition migration: read task b5vn147ag (flow_a_uc_reference + flow_b_medallion reseed runs). If both success: check the DataQ side — the trigger-bound flow_a_uc_reference suite run should have fired on DAG success (list recent runs via the refreshed bearer), and trigger a manual run of the flow_b_gold suite if none fired; verify runs succeeded. Then: tick all Phase 3/4 boxes in ~/Coding/Python/DataQ-harness/DATABRICKS_MIGRATION_TODO.md, update memory project_live_connections_and_demo.md (new workspace dbc-4492dde4-090c, warehouse b6403b6e3734f0ce, 3 Free-Edition PATs minted 2026-07-03 ~90d, Free Edition constraints: PATs work, warehouse config locked 2X-Small/auto-stop-10, catalogs via SQL not TF-create, non-commercial), update HARNESS_TODO.md pointer if it references the old workspace, and deliver the final migration report. If a DAG failed, inspect its task logs via the Airflow API and iterate.

state: SUCCEEDED
result: [['positive', 'negative']]

1	"""Flow B — medallion (bronze -> silver -> gold) in Unity Catalog. §3b.
2	
3	Bronze NDJSON the §2a mockdata Jobs drop in ADLS is loaded into UC `raw` (worker reads
4	with pandas, writes via the Databricks SQL warehouse — same path as the reference loads);
5	silver/gold are SQL transforms on the warehouse. Implemented on the **serverless SQL
6	warehouse** rather than a PySpark job cluster: the trial workspace's reliable compute is
7	serverless, and reading ADLS from the warehouse is avoided by loading raw in the worker
8	(no UC storage credential for ADLS — that needs a role assignment the harness SP can't make).
9	
10	Pure ETL — DataQ discovers this run by polling the Airflow REST API, not via any push.
11	
12	Required env (key-based): AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING,
13	DATABRICKS_HOST/TOKEN/HTTP_PATH, UC_CATALOG.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import os
19	
20	import pendulum
21	from _harness import load_to_uc, read_feed, run_uc_sql
22	from airflow import DAG
23	from airflow.operators.python import PythonOperator
24	from dataq_airflow_callback import on_dataq_failure, on_dataq_success
25	
26	CATALOG = os.environ.get("UC_CATALOG", "dataq_retail")
27	
28	
29	def _bronze(**_: object) -> None:
30	    """Load the raw NDJSON bronze sources into UC raw.* (schema-on-read)."""
31	    for feed in ("sales_events", "feedback"):
32	        df = read_feed(f"databricks_medallion/bronze/{feed}", "json")
33	        if not df.empty:
34	            load_to_uc(df, CATALOG, "raw", feed)
35	
36	
37	def _silver(**_: object) -> None:
38	    """Clean + PII-redact (sha2 the feedback email — #226); dedup; project sales."""
39	    run_uc_sql(
40	        [
41	            f"""CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE `{CATALOG}`.`silver`.`feedback` AS
42	                SELECT feedback_id, customer_id, channel, rating, comment,
43	                       sha2(CAST(email AS STRING), 256) AS email, created_ts
44	                FROM `{CATALOG}`.`raw`.`feedback`
45	                QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY feedback_id ORDER BY created_ts) = 1""",
46	            f"""CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE `{CATALOG}`.`silver`.`sales` AS
47	                SELECT order_number, channel, store_id,
48	                       CAST(amount AS DOUBLE) AS amount,
49	                       to_date(event_ts) AS sale_date
50	                FROM `{CATALOG}`.`raw`.`sales_events`
51	                WHERE event_type = 'placed'""",
52	        ]
53	    )
54	
55	
56	def _gold(**_: object) -> None:
57	    """Daily revenue rollup — reconciles back to Snowflake order_total (ADR 0014)."""
58	    run_uc_sql(
59	        [
60	            f"""CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE `{CATALOG}`.`gold`.`daily_revenue` AS
61	                SELECT sale_date, channel,
62	                       SUM(amount) AS daily_revenue,
63	                       COUNT(DISTINCT order_number) AS orders
64	                FROM `{CATALOG}`.`silver`.`sales`
65	                GROUP BY sale_date, channel""",
66	        ]
67	    )
68	
69	
70	with DAG(
71	    default_args={
72	        # Retry transient failures (serverless warehouse cold start) with backoff.
73	        "retries": 4,
74	        "retry_delay": pendulum.duration(seconds=30),
75	        "retry_exponential_backoff": True,
76	        "max_retry_delay": pendulum.duration(minutes=5),
77	    },
78	    dag_id="flow_b_medallion",
79	    description="DataQ harness Flow B — UC bronze->silver->gold medallion (SQL warehouse)",
80	    schedule="0 2 * * *",  # after the Flow A loads
81	    start_date=pendulum.datetime(2026, 6, 1, tz="UTC"),
82	    catchup=False,
83	    # Notify DataQ on every run completion (success + failure); success fires the
84	    # bound suite. Fail-safe — a callback error never affects the DAG run.
85	    on_success_callback=on_dataq_success,
86	    on_failure_callback=on_dataq_failure,
87	    tags=["dataq-harness", "flow-b", "medallion"],
88	):
89	    bronze = PythonOperator(task_id="bronze_raw_load", python_callable=_bronze)
90	    silver = PythonOperator(task_id="silver_clean_pii", python_callable=_silver)
91	    gold = PythonOperator(task_id="gold_daily_revenue", python_callable=_gold)
92	    bronze >> silver >> gold

# mockdata image — runs as the Container Apps Job (cron) that produces files into
# the ADLS landing zone (§2a "file creation" layer). Same Python runtime as DataQ (3.13).
#
# Build (from harness root):  docker build -t mockdata:latest .
# Local smoke:                docker run --rm -v "$PWD/out:/data/out" -v "$PWD/state:/data/state" \
#                               mockdata:latest init --out /data/out --state /data/state
# Job invocation (per feed):  ... tick --feed inventory --out adls://<acct>/landing --state adls://<acct>/landing/state

FROM python:3.13-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Deps first for layer caching. pyarrow/pandas wheels are prebuilt for slim.
COPY mockdata/requirements.txt mockdata/requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r mockdata/requirements.txt

# The package itself.
COPY mockdata/ mockdata/

# `python -m mockdata <subcommand>` — args supplied by the Job's command/args.
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "-m", "mockdata"]
CMD ["--help"]

input.new_string:
def _gold(**_: object) -> None:
    """Daily revenue rollup — reconciles back to Snowflake order_total (ADR 0014)."""
    run_uc_sql(
        [
            f"""CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE `{CATALOG}`.`gold`.`daily_revenue` AS
                SELECT sale_date, channel,
                       SUM(amount) AS daily_revenue,
                       COUNT(DISTINCT order_number) AS orders
                FROM `{CATALOG}`.`silver`.`sales`
                GROUP BY sale_date, channel""",
        ]
    )


def _gold_sentiment(**_: object) -> None:
    """AI-scored feedback sentiment (gold). INCREMENTAL on purpose, twice over:

    - `ai_analyze_sentiment` spends model-serving quota per row (Free Edition
      fair-use pauses compute for the DAY when exhausted), so only rows not
      already scored are sent to the model — a re-run scores nothing.
    - MERGE (not CREATE OR REPLACE like the other gold step): rebuilding would
      re-score the whole history every night for identical output.

    `sentiment` is the AI verdict (positive|negative|neutral|mixed); `rating`
    rides along so DataQ can cross-check the model against the 1–5 stars.
    """
    run_uc_sql(
        [
            f"""CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `{CATALOG}`.`gold`.`feedback_sentiment` (
                    feedback_id STRING,
                    customer_id STRING,
                    channel STRING,
                    rating BIGINT,
                    sentiment STRING,
                    created_ts STRING,
                    scored_at TIMESTAMP)""",
            f"""MERGE INTO `{CATALOG}`.`gold`.`feedback_sentiment` t
                USING (
                    SELECT s.feedback_id, s.customer_id, s.channel, s.rating,
                           ai_analyze_sentiment(s.comment) AS sentiment,
                           s.created_ts, current_timestamp() AS scored_at
                    FROM `{CATALOG}`.`silver`.`feedback` s
                    LEFT ANTI JOIN `{CATALOG}`.`gold`.`feedback_sentiment` g
                        ON s.feedback_id = g.feedback_id
                ) n
                ON t.feedback_id = n.feedback_id
                WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *""",
        ]
    )

bash <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
AF="https://dataq-harness-airflow.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io"
PW=$(az keyvault secret show --vault-name dataq-app-kv-aw6laj -n conn-airflow --query value -o tsv)
# wait for the webserver to come back + the DAG to carry the new task
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  TASKS=$(curl -s -u "admin:$PW" "$AF/api/v1/dags/flow_b_medallion/tasks" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(' '.join(t['task_id'] for t in json.load(sys.stdin).get('tasks',[])))" 2>/dev/null || true)
  echo "poll $i: tasks=[$TASKS]"
  case "$TASKS" in *gold_feedback_sentiment*) break;; esac
  sleep 20
done
case "$TASKS" in *gold_feedback_sentiment*) ;; *) echo "new task never appeared"; exit 1;; esac
RUN=$(curl -s -u "admin:$PW" -X POST "$AF/api/v1/dags/flow_b_medallion/dagRuns" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"conf":{}}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('dag_run_id',''))")
echo "triggered: $RUN"
for i in $(seq 1 25); do
  sleep 20
  STATE=$(curl -s -u "admin:$PW" "$AF/api/v1/dags/flow_b_medallion/dagRuns/$RUN" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('state',''))")
  echo "run poll $i: $STATE"
  [ "$STATE" = "success" ] && exit 0
  [ "$STATE" = "failed" ] && exit 1
done
exit 1
EOF

result.output:
1:"""Resolve a suite's datasource-shaped target to the runner's (table, schema, catalog).
2-
3-A suite's `target` (#215) is a small JSONB document shaped like the column
4:profiler request (``table`` / ``schema`` / ``catalog`` / ``path`` /
5-``file_format``), datasource-typed. The `CheckRunner` interface is *table-shaped*
6-— for a flat-file datasource the file path rides the ``table`` argument
7-(``flatfile.py``) — so every datasource resolves to the same triple the worker
8-hands to ``run_service.execute_run`` and ``build_check_runner``:
9-
10-    snowflake      → table (+ schema)
11:    unity_catalog  → table (+ schema) + catalog        (catalog.schema.table)
12:    adls_gen2 / s3 → path  (carried as `table`; schema/catalog unused)
13-
14-A flat-file target can instead be a **batch** spec — files arrive in batches
15-(``orders_2026-06-01.csv`` …) and a run targets one of them: ``pattern`` (a regex
16-whose first capture group is the batch key) + ``strategy`` (``latest`` /
17-``specific``, with ``batch`` for ``specific``) + an optional ``prefix`` to list
18-under. The concrete path can only be known by *listing the store*, so it's
19-resolved at run time (`materialize_path`), not at save time.
20-
21-Resolution is two-phase so write-time validation stays pure (no network, no GX):
22-
--
70-class ResolvedTarget:
71-    """The runner inputs a suite resolves to. ``table`` carries the file path for
72:    flat-file datasources; ``catalog`` is set only for Unity Catalog. ``batch`` is
73-    set only for a flat-file *batch* target, in which case ``table`` is empty until
74-    `materialize_path` lists the store and resolves the concrete path."""
75-
76-    table: str

    update = _maybe_enrich(provider_impl, connection, update, secret_store)

        session, provider=provider, connection=connection, update=update
    )
    triggered = (
        _trigger_suites(session, provider=provider, connection=connection, update=update)

        else []

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PollIngestResult:
    pipeline_runs: list[PipelineRun] = field(default_factory=list)
    triggered_runs: list[Run] = field(default_factory=list)
    skipped: int = 0


def ingest_polled_runs(
    session: Session,
    *,
    provider_impl: OrchestrationProvider,
    connection: Connection,
    updates: list[RunUpdate],
    skip_updated_since: datetime,
) -> PollIngestResult:
    """Persist the runs a poll returned for one orchestrator connection.

    Records **every status** for the monitor view (#490), but stays the
    **trigger-on-success** channel (ADR 0004): only a ``succeeded`` run triggers a
    suite — failures/running are recorded, never triggered (mirrors `ingest_event`).
    Poll data is already authoritative, so there is no REST enrichment.

    The ``skip_updated_since`` churn-optimisation skips a run we already recorded
    inside this window — but **only when the existing row is already terminal**
    (succeeded/failed/cancelled). A non-terminal row (queued/running) must always
    be re-processed: now that the poll records non-terminal states (#490), skipping
    it on time alone would drop a later ``running → succeeded`` transition — losing
    both the monitor update *and* the trigger. The connection is known (we polled
    it), so no resolve.
    """
    provider = provider_impl.provider
    pipeline_runs: list[PipelineRun] = []
    triggered: list[Run] = []
    skipped = 0
    for update in updates:
        existing = session.execute(
            select(PipelineRun.status, PipelineRun.last_updated_at).where(
                PipelineRun.provider == provider,
                PipelineRun.provider_run_id == update.provider_run_id,
            )
        ).first()
        if (
            existing is not None
            and existing.status in _TERMINAL_PIPELINE_STATUSES
            and existing.last_updated_at >= skip_updated_since
        ):
            skipped += 1
            continue
        pipeline_runs.append(
            _upsert_pipeline_run(session, provider=provider, connection=connection, update=update)

content.text:
**Done — the feedback sentiment layer now exists end-to-end, and the full chain is verified green.** The trigger-bound run (`67cd44f4`) passed **4/4 checks** with 0% unexpected rows.

To answer your original question honestly: it wasn't in any tracker — no mention of sentiment analysis in `HARNESS_TODO` (since retired), the roadmap, docs, or DAGs; feedback data previously dead-ended at silver. So I built it as a proper Flow B extension:

**The pipeline** (`flow_b_medallion`, now 4 stages): bronze → silver → gold revenue → **`gold_feedback_sentiment`** — a new task that scores `silver.feedback.comment` with Databricks' `ai_analyze_sentiment()` (verified working on Free Edition first). It's deliberately **incremental** (MERGE with an anti-join, only unscored rows hit the model) — both to avoid re-burning the fair-use quota nightly and to avoid re-scoring identical history. Shipped as Airflow image `callback-2` (DAGs are baked in), rolled via terraform.

**The DataQ side**: new suite **"Feedback Sentiment — UC gold"** (owned by Olivia) with 4 checks — `sentiment` not-null, `sentiment` in {positive, negative, neutral, mixed}, `rating` between 1–5, and volume ≥ 1 — plus a **trigger binding** on `(airflow, flow_b_medallion, dev)`. That makes it the second suite bound to Flow B (the dedup index is per-suite, so both fire), and tonight's run proved it: DAG success → callback/poll ingest → auto-triggered suite run → **succeeded, all checks pass**.

It's a nice demo arc now: an *AI-derived* gold table with DQ guardrails on the model's output shape — if the model starts emitting junk labels or the feedback feed stalls, DataQ alerts. for i in $(seq 1 20); do

  [[ "$STATE" == "success" || "$STATE" == "failed" ]] && break
done
[[ "$STATE" == "success" ]]
EOF

Yes — checked against the current Free Edition limitations doc (updated 2026-06-30), and **everything our UC lane does fits**, with one thing to verify at signup and one policy caveat. Here's the workload-by-workload mapping:

| Our workload | What it needs | Free Edition says | Fits? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `flow_a_uc_reference` + `flow_b_medallion` DAGs | Airflow worker connects **in** via `databricks-sql-connector` to one SQL warehouse | One serverless SQL warehouse, **2X-Small** | ✅ — one warehouse is all we use, and 2X-Small is the downsize I recommended anyway |
| Flow B notebook step (RAW→Silver→Gold) | One job run at a time on serverless | Jobs capped at **5 concurrent tasks** | ✅ — we'd use 1 |
| DataQ UC connections (retail + qa): suite runs, dry-run, profiler | JDBC-style SQL over the same warehouse | Warehouse + workspace APIs available; only **account-level** APIs are off | ✅ — config-only change on DataQ's side (host/http_path/warehouse_id), no code |
| UC catalog/schemas/tables (retail model) | Create catalog + managed tables | One workspace + one metastore; managed storage only | ✅ — we don't use external storage; data arrives via INSERTs from the Airflow worker, so the restricted *outbound* internet doesn't touch us |
| Nightly usage volume | ~15–30 min of 2X-Small per day | Fair-use quota; compute pauses for the day if exceeded | ✅ — our footprint is tiny; heavy smoke days might hit the cap, scheduled load won't |
| R/Scala, custom compute, private networking | — | Unsupported | ✅ not used |

**The one thing to verify at signup:** the docs don't explicitly confirm **PAT support** — they restrict only account-level APIs and console auth methods (email OTP / Google / Microsoft), and workspace-level tooling is a headline use case, so PATs should work — but since every integration we have (Airflow env, DataQ secrets, my rotation recipe) authenticates with PATs, I'd create the account and mint a token as the very first step before committing to the migration. Two minutes to confirm.

**The policy caveat:** Free Edition is **non-commercial use only**, with no SLA and possible deletion after prolonged inactivity. For a portfolio/demo project that's fine; if DataQ's demo ever fronts anything commercial, it isn't.

**Migration effort if you go ahead** (~2–3 hours, mostly scripted already): new workspace → mint PATs → recreate the retail catalog/schemas + reseed (mockdata + the untracked notebook) → update `DATABRICKS_HOST`/`HTTP_PATH`/warehouse_id in harness tfvars + `secrets.sh` + the two Airflow ACA env entries → update the 3 KV secrets + the two DataQ connection configs → unpause the DAGs and re-run the Flow B verification.

Sources: [Databricks Free Edition limitations (Azure)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/getting-started/free-edition-limitations), [Databricks Free Edition limitations (AWS)](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/getting-started/free-edition-limitations)

Want me to hold here (jobs stay paused, everything else keeps running), or should I prep the migration plan/scripts so it's a fast cutover once you've created the Free Edition account and we've confirmed the PAT check?

done
EOF

content.text:
**Migration complete — the UC lane is fully back on Databricks Free Edition at $0, end-to-end verified.** The final proof came straight from the prod DB: at **22:47:11Z** both trigger-bound DataQ suite runs (`airflow:flow_a_uc_reference:…` and `airflow:flow_b_medallion:…`) **succeeded** against the new warehouse — DAG load → success callback → DataQ ingest → auto-triggered GX suite run, the whole chain on the new workspace.

The full journey, as you sequenced it:

1. **Backup** — turned out minimal by design: no notebooks or Databricks jobs existed (Flow B is pure SQL from Airflow), so I dumped the 13 tables' column schemas + configs to `databricks/backup-2026-07-02/`; data was regenerable, DataQ's history lives in its own Postgres.
2. **Cancel + teardown** — nothing billable existed ($0 trial); you cancelled the plan and deleted the workspace; I cleaned the 8 dead resources out of the harness terraform state.
3. **Free Edition + PAT gate** — new workspace `dbc-4492dde4-090c`, CLI OAuth profile `dataq-free`, and the decisive check passed: **Free Edition mints PATs**.
4. **Replicate** — 3 dedicated 90-day PATs → the 3 KV secrets; one Free Edition quirk handled: Default Storage rejects catalog creation via TF/API, so `dataq_retail` + 4 schemas went in **via SQL** then `terraform import` — the harness apply is now fully clean (grants included) and rolled both Airflow apps to the new host/path/token; both DataQ connections PATCHed and testing `{ok:true}`.
5. **Resume** — DAGs unpaused, reseed runs green (dims + raw→silver→gold rebuilt), schedules live again, and the trigger-bound DataQ runs above sealed the verification.

Worth remembering: the warehouse is the fixed 2X-Small with config locked (auto-stop stays 10 min — irrelevant at $0), the fair-use quota pauses compute for the *day* if a heavy smoke session blows through it, and the account is non-commercial-only. Everything — new host, PAT expiries (~2026-09-30), the SQL-not-TF catalog quirk, the CLI profile — is recorded in memory and the closed `DATABRICKS_MIGRATION_TODO.md`.

One loose end from earlier tonight for whenever you feel like it: the optional Databricks account-record deletion ticket at help.databricks.com (the old plan is cancelled and bills nothing either way).

input.description: Check UC suite-target shape

result.output:
1:"""Resolve a suite's datasource-shaped target to the runner's (table, schema, catalog).
2-
3-A suite's `target` (#215) is a small JSONB document shaped like the column
4:profiler request (``table`` / ``schema`` / ``catalog`` / ``path`` /
5-``file_format``), datasource-typed. The `CheckRunner` interface is *table-shaped*
6-— for a flat-file datasource the file path rides the ``table`` argument
7-(``flatfile.py``) — so every datasource resolves to the same triple the worker
8-hands to ``run_service.execute_run`` and ``build_check_runner``:
9-
10-    snowflake      → table (+ schema)
11:    unity_catalog  → table (+ schema) + catalog        (catalog.schema.table)
12:    adls_gen2 / s3 → path  (carried as `table`; schema/catalog unused)
13-
14-A flat-file target can instead be a **batch** spec — files arrive in batches
15-(``orders_2026-06-01.csv`` …) and a run targets one of them: ``pattern`` (a regex
16-whose first capture group is the batch key) + ``strategy`` (``latest`` /
17-``specific``, with ``batch`` for ``specific``) + an optional ``prefix`` to list
18-under. The concrete path can only be known by *listing the store*, so it's
19-resolved at run time (`materialize_path`), not at save time.
20-
21-Resolution is two-phase so write-time validation stays pure (no network, no GX):
22-
--
70-class ResolvedTarget:
71-    """The runner inputs a suite resolves to. ``table`` carries the file path for
72:    flat-file datasources; ``catalog`` is set only for Unity Catalog. ``batch`` is
73-    set only for a flat-file *batch* target, in which case ``table`` is empty until
74-    `materialize_path` lists the store and resolves the concrete path."""
75-
76-    table: str

set -euo pipefail

import json

u=[v for k,v in d.items() if k.startswith('oidc.user:')][0]
print(json.loads(u).get('refresh_token',''))")
AT=$(curl -s -X POST "https://login.microsoftonline.com/07b592a3-12ee-4631-abb1-8a7b73d91241/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
  -H "Origin: https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io" \
  -d "client_id=09214fa7-5978-4739-a46b-cfb669464a22&grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=$RT" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('access_token',''))")
B=https://dataq-app-frontend.purplefield-f7322a1b.westus2.azurecontainerapps.io/api/v1
SUITE=$(curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $AT" -H "Content-Type: application/json" "$B/suites" -d '{
  "name": "Feedback Sentiment — UC gold",
  "description": "DQ over the AI-scored feedback sentiment table (ai_analyze_sentiment, Flow B gold). Guards the model output shape + the feed volume.",
  "connection_id": "ae7b09b7-4114-41f3-814d-849c25184dbc",
  "target": {"catalog": "dataq_retail", "schema": "gold", "table": "feedback_sentiment"}
}')
SID=$(printf '%s' "$SUITE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))")
[[ -n "$SID" ]] || { echo "suite create failed:"; printf '%s' "$SUITE" | head -c 300; exit 1; }
echo "suite: $SID"
mkchk() {
  curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $AT" -H "Content-Type: application/json" "$B/suites/$SID/checks" -d "$1" \
    | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('  check:', d.get('name', d.get('error',{}).get('code')), d.get('id','')[:8])"
}
mkchk '{"name": "sentiment not null", "expectation_type": "expect_column_values_to_not_be_null", "config": {"column": "sentiment"}}'

mkchk '{"name": "rating 1-5", "expectation_type": "expect_column_values_to_be_between", "config": {"column": "rating", "min_value": 1, "max_value": 5}}'
mkchk '{"name": "feedback volume >= 1", "expectation_type": "expect_table_row_count_to_be_between", "config": {"min_value": 1}}'
BIND=$(curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Bearer $AT" -H "Content-Type: application/json" "$B/trigger-bindings" -d "{
  \"provider\": \"airflow\", \"pipeline_or_dag_id\": \"flow_b_medallion\", \"env\": \"dev\", \"suite_id\": \"$SID\"}")
echo "binding: $(printf '%s' "$BIND" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('id', d.get('error',{}).get('code','?')))")"
echo "$SID" > /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-arijit-Coding-Python-DataQ/a33e4939-e8b1-4b81-aaa1-168fd7fc382f/scratchpad/sentiment_suite_id
EOF

   - `ingest_polled_runs` skip semantics: only terminal rows within window are skipped; running→succeeded transitions always re-processed and trigger

38-## Known limitations (v1)
39-
40-- **GX-only** check engine (Databricks DQX deferred to v1.1); batch-oriented (not
41-  streaming).
42-- **Single tenant**, suite-level access sharing; workspace-admin is a config allowlist.
43-- Interactive **datasource browsing** (container browser, 3-level UC catalog picker) is
44-  deferred — you specify targets explicitly. JSON flat files deferred (CSV/Parquet in v1).
45-- Auth is **Azure AD only** (no API keys / service tokens yet — see ADR 0026 / #461).

47-## FAQ
48-
49-**Is ADF/Airflow a datasource?** No — they're orchestration providers DataQ monitors and
50-can trigger from. You never write checks against them. See **[Concepts](concepts.md)**.
51-
52-**Do I need Azure to run it locally?** No — local dev uses a dev-bypass auth and
53-docker-compose. Azure is one deployment target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013).
54-
55-**Where do failed-row samples go?** Stored with the result, **PII-redacted**, and purged
56-after a retention window — never written to logs.
57-
58:**Can an AI assistant use DataQ?** Yes — 8 MCP tools at `/mcp` (Claude Desktop / Claude.ai
59-/ Copilot / Cursor), Azure-AD authenticated. See the README's MCP section.

35-## Quick start
36-
37-### Run DataQ — prebuilt images (recommended)
38-
39-Evaluate or self-host in ~5 minutes: **no source checkout, no Azure tenant.** Just Docker.
40-
41-```bash
42-curl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/main/docker-compose.ghcr.yml
43-docker compose -f docker-compose.ghcr.yml up
44-```
45-
46-Open **<http://localhost:3000>** — the stack comes up migrated and seeded with demo data, on **dev-bypass auth** (every request is a fixed demo user; no sign-in). API + Swagger at `http://localhost:8000/docs`. Images are pulled from GHCR and are **multi-arch** (amd64 + arm64), so Apple-Silicon runs native. Ports bind to `127.0.0.1` only. To pin a release instead of the moving tags: `DATAQ_BACKEND_TAG=vX.Y.Z DATAQ_FRONTEND_TAG=vX.Y.Z docker compose -f docker-compose.ghcr.yml up`.
47-
48-> Self-hosting with **your own** Azure AD? The published frontend is **one generic image** — the compose eval runs it with `DATAQ_AUTH_MODE=bypass` (auth off). For real SSO, **no rebuild**: run that same image with `DATAQ_AUTH_MODE=oidc` + `DATAQ_AUTH_AUTHORITY` / `DATAQ_AUTH_CLIENT_ID` / `DATAQ_AUTH_API_SCOPE` (auth config is injected at runtime, ADR 0028), and run the backend with `AUTH_DEV_BYPASS` off. See [Getting started](https://theurgicduke771.github.io/DataQ/getting-started/).
49-
50-### Develop DataQ — from source
51-
52-```bash
53-git clone https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ.git
54-cd DataQ
55-./scripts/setup.sh       # conda env + pre-commit + docker-compose + migrations
56-conda activate dataq
57-docker-compose up
58-```
59-
60-Backend at `http://localhost:8000` (Swagger at `/docs`), frontend at `http://localhost:3000`.
61-
62:## MCP (AI assistant access)
63-
64:DataQ exposes 8 curated MCP tools at `/mcp` (streamable HTTP) — `list_suites`, `get_suite_results`, `get_health_score`, `get_adf_pipeline_status`, `trigger_suite_run`, `get_run_status`, `create_check`, `profile_column`. The endpoint is **Azure AD–protected**: present the same bearer token the web UI uses (validated against the same tenant / audience / scope). Without Azure auth configured the endpoint is only mounted in local dev-bypass mode — never unauthenticated in a deployed environment (ADR [0008](docs/adr/0008-mcp-server.md)).
65-
66:Point any MCP client at `https://<your-dataq-host>/mcp` with an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
67-
68:**Claude Desktop / Claude.ai** (`claude_desktop_config.json`) — and **GitHub Copilot** (`mcp.json`):
69-

71-{
72:  "mcpServers": {
73-    "dataq": {
74:      "url": "https://<your-dataq-host>/mcp",
75-      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer <AZURE_AD_ACCESS_TOKEN>" }
76-    }
77-  }
78-}
79-```
80-
81:**Cursor** (`~/.cursor/mcp.json`) uses the same `mcpServers` shape. Once configured, all 8 tools are available to natural-language queries (e.g. *"what failed in the orders suite today?"*, *"run the orders suite on DEV"*).

Daily Celery-beat `purge_sample_failures` → `run_service.purge_expired_sample_failures` scrubs `sample_failures` (the only potentially-PII result column) to SQL NULL + stamps `sample_failures_purged_at` on results older than the configurable `sample_failures_retention_days` (default 30). Honoured the pre-built seam (the column + setting + the three "retention sweep" comments existed with no writer). Targets only real sample *objects* via `jsonb_typeof` (the JSONB column stores Python `None` as JSON `'null'`, so `IS NOT NULL` over-matches every passing/errored row); naturally idempotent (scrubbed → SQL NULL → excluded); `retention_days <= 0` disables. 6 DB-backed sweep tests + 1 task-wiring unit test. **(A8)**

input.old_string:
- `key_vault_purge_protection` is off during bring-up (so a destroy/re-apply can
  reuse the vault name); set it **true** for a hardened prod (irreversible).
input.new_string:
- `key_vault_purge_protection` is off during bring-up (so a destroy/re-apply can
  reuse the vault name); set it **true** for a hardened prod (irreversible).
  **Decision (2026-07-02): deliberately left off** for this deployment — the vault
  is demo/trial-scoped and destroy/re-apply flexibility wins; every secret it holds
  (PATs, SAS, webhook secrets) is rotatable, so accidental-delete recovery is
  re-mint, not data loss. Revisit (flip to `true`) before any regulated or
  production-critical use.
